text: Here's what science actually says about p values nuance. P values determine if results are statistically significant, but the story doesn’t end there. A p value below 0.05 often gets all the attention, but it’s not a magic threshold. It can’t tell us how important or relevant a finding is—only if it’s unlikely to occur by chance. So, when you hear p values, remember: context matters, and results can always vary. Want to dive deeper into statistics? Hit subscribe for more insights!
